verses.text: The Pharisees ask, 'Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife?' tempting Him. A baited question. They hope to trap Him on the law.
The Pharisees ask, 'Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife?' tempting Him. A baited question. They hope to trap Him on the law.
Moses suffered divorce 'for the hardness of your heart.' Concession. The permission was for sin, not God's true will.
Moses suffered divorce 'for the hardness of your heart.' Concession. The permission was for sin, not God's true will.
Moses suffered divorce 'for the hardness of your heart.' Concession. The permission was for sin, not God's true will.
From the beginning 'they twain shall be one flesh'; 'what... God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.' Marriage from creation. God binds husband and wife as one.
From the beginning 'they twain shall be one flesh'; 'what... God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.' Marriage from creation. God binds husband and wife as one.
From the beginning 'they twain shall be one flesh'; 'what... God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.' Marriage from creation. God binds husband and wife as one.
From the beginning 'they twain shall be one flesh'; 'what... God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.' Marriage from creation. God binds husband and wife as one.
Whoso puts away his wife and marries another commits adultery. Faithfulness. The marriage bond is sacred and lifelong.
Whoso puts away his wife and marries another commits adultery. Faithfulness. The marriage bond is sacred and lifelong.
Whoso puts away his wife and marries another commits adultery. Faithfulness. The marriage bond is sacred and lifelong.
Children are brought; the disciples rebuke them; 'Suffer the little children to come unto me.' Welcomed. Christ has room and love for the youngest.
Children are brought; the disciples rebuke them; 'Suffer the little children to come unto me.' Welcomed. Christ has room and love for the youngest.
Whoso receives not the kingdom 'as a little child' shall not enter; He takes them up and blesses them. Childlike faith. The kingdom is entered by humble, trusting hearts.
Whoso receives not the kingdom 'as a little child' shall not enter; He takes them up and blesses them. Childlike faith. The kingdom is entered by humble, trusting hearts.
A man runs, 'Good Master, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?' He names the commandments. The law's mirror. He points the seeker to God's demands.
A man runs, 'Good Master, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?' He names the commandments. The law's mirror. He points the seeker to God's demands.
A man runs, 'Good Master, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?' He names the commandments. The law's mirror. He points the seeker to God's demands.
Claiming to keep all, he is loved and told, 'sell... give to the poor... follow me'; he goes away grieved, being rich. Riches refused. His wealth held him from Christ.
Claiming to keep all, he is loved and told, 'sell... give to the poor... follow me'; he goes away grieved, being rich. Riches refused. His wealth held him from Christ.
Claiming to keep all, he is loved and told, 'sell... give to the poor... follow me'; he goes away grieved, being rich. Riches refused. His wealth held him from Christ.
'How hardly shall they that have riches enter the kingdom!' easier a camel through a needle. Hard for the rich. Wealth easily becomes a snare to the soul.
'How hardly shall they that have riches enter the kingdom!' easier a camel through a needle. Hard for the rich. Wealth easily becomes a snare to the soul.
'How hardly shall they that have riches enter the kingdom!' easier a camel through a needle. Hard for the rich. Wealth easily becomes a snare to the soul.
Who then can be saved? 'with men it is impossible, but not with God: for with God all things are possible.' Grace alone. Salvation is God's work, beyond man's power.
Who then can be saved? 'with men it is impossible, but not with God: for with God all things are possible.' Grace alone. Salvation is God's work, beyond man's power.
We have left all; none who forsakes for Christ but receives a hundredfold, 'with persecutions,' and eternal life. Rich reward. No loss for Christ goes unrepaid.
We have left all; none who forsakes for Christ but receives a hundredfold, 'with persecutions,' and eternal life. Rich reward. No loss for Christ goes unrepaid.
We have left all; none who forsakes for Christ but receives a hundredfold, 'with persecutions,' and eternal life. Rich reward. No loss for Christ goes unrepaid.
We have left all; none who forsakes for Christ but receives a hundredfold, 'with persecutions,' and eternal life. Rich reward. No loss for Christ goes unrepaid.
Going up to Jerusalem, He foretells His mocking, scourging, death, and rising. Plain warning. He walks knowingly toward the cross.
Going up to Jerusalem, He foretells His mocking, scourging, death, and rising. Plain warning. He walks knowingly toward the cross.
Going up to Jerusalem, He foretells His mocking, scourging, death, and rising. Plain warning. He walks knowingly toward the cross.
James and John ask to sit on His right and left in glory. Misplaced ambition. They seek thrones while He speaks of death.
James and John ask to sit on His right and left in glory. Misplaced ambition. They seek thrones while He speaks of death.
James and John ask to sit on His right and left in glory. Misplaced ambition. They seek thrones while He speaks of death.
Can ye drink My cup? ye shall, but the places are the Father's to give. The way of suffering. Glory comes by the cup of sorrow, not by request.
Can ye drink My cup? ye shall, but the places are the Father's to give. The way of suffering. Glory comes by the cup of sorrow, not by request.
Can ye drink My cup? ye shall, but the places are the Father's to give. The way of suffering. Glory comes by the cup of sorrow, not by request.
Greatness is in serving; 'the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.' The great pattern. Christ's whole life was self-giving service.
Greatness is in serving; 'the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.' The great pattern. Christ's whole life was self-giving service.

Blind Bartimaeus cries, 'Jesus, thou Son of David, have mercy'; rebuked, he cries the more. Persistent faith. He will not be hushed from seeking Christ.
Blind Bartimaeus cries, 'Jesus, thou Son of David, have mercy'; rebuked, he cries the more. Persistent faith. He will not be hushed from seeking Christ.
Blind Bartimaeus cries, 'Jesus, thou Son of David, have mercy'; rebuked, he cries the more. Persistent faith. He will not be hushed from seeking Christ.
Blind Bartimaeus cries, 'Jesus, thou Son of David, have mercy'; rebuked, he cries the more. Persistent faith. He will not be hushed from seeking Christ.
Casting off his garment, he asks his sight; 'thy faith hath made thee whole'; he sees and follows. Sight and discipleship. The healed at once becomes a follower.
